interpolators.name: interpolateAny
interpolators.description: Fallback interpolator factory that snaps from the first value to the second at the halfway point.

interpolators.name: interpolateColor
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that smoothly transitions between two CSS color strings by interpolating their RGBA channels.

interpolators.name: interpolateDate
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that interpolates between two `Date` instances by lerping their timestamps.

interpolators.name: interpolateGradient
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that transitions between two CSS gradient strings by interpolating their stops, angles, and positions.

interpolators.name: interpolateNumber
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that linearly interpolates between two numbers.

interpolators.name: interpolateWaypoint
interpolators.description: Creates an interpolator that returns the point along a polyline at the given normalised position.

interpolators.name: interpolatePath
interpolators.description: Creates an interpolator that progressively reveals a path from start to end as position advances from 0 to 1.

interpolators.description: Creates an interpolator that traces a point around the vertices of a regular polygon.
interpolators.typeSignature:
function interpolatePolygonPoint(
    sides: number,
    cx: number,
    cy: number,
    radius: number,
    closePath: boolean = true
): Interpolator<Point>

interpolators.name: interpolateCirclePoint
interpolators.description: Creates an interpolator that traces a point around a circle of the given centre and radius.

interpolators.name: interpolatePoints
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that transitions between two point arrays, extrapolating additional points where set lengths differ.

interpolators.name: interpolateBorderRadius
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that transitions between two border-radius values (single number or four-corner tuple).

interpolators.name: interpolateRotation
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that transitions between two rotation values (numbers in radians or strings like `"90deg"`).

interpolators.name: interpolateString
interpolators.description: Creates a string interpolator by interpolating between numeric values embedded in tagged template literals.
interpolators.typeSignature: function interpolateString(callback: (tag: typeof tagIntStr) => StringInterpolationSet, formatter?: StringInterpolationFormatter): Interpolator<string>

interpolators.name: StringInterpolationSet
interpolators.description: A pair of tagged template results representing the start and end states for string interpolation.

interpolators.name: StringInterpolationFormatter
interpolators.description: Optional formatter applied to each interpolated numeric value before insertion into the output string.

interpolators.name: StringInterpolatorTag
interpolators.description: A tagged template result capturing the static fragments and dynamic numeric arguments.

interpolators.name: interpolateTransformOrigin
interpolators.description: Interpolator factory that transitions between two transform-origin values (numbers or percentage strings).

interpolators.name: Interpolator
interpolators.description: A function that interpolates between two values based on a normalised position (0–1).

interpolators.name: PredicatedFunction
interpolators.description: A callable with a `test` method used to determine whether the factory can handle a given value.

interpolators.name: InterpolatorFactory
interpolators.description: A factory that creates an interpolator between two values of the same type, with a `test` predicate for type matching.

scales.name: createScale
scales.description: Assembles a `Scale` object from explicit conversion, inversion, and tick functions.
scales.typeSignature: function createScale<TDomain = number, TRange = number>(options: ScaleBindingOptions<TDomain, TRange>): Scale<TDomain, TRange>

scales.name: getLinearScaleMethod
scales.description: Creates a linear mapping function from a numeric domain to a numeric range, with optional clamping and tick-padding.
scales.typeSignature: function getLinearScaleMethod(domain: number[], range: number[], options?: LinearScaleOptions): ScaleMethod

scales.name: createNumericIncludesMethod
scales.description: Creates an `includes` predicate that tests whether a value falls within the numeric domain.
scales.typeSignature: function createNumericIncludesMethod(domain: number[])

scales.name: getLinearTicks
scales.description: Generates an array of evenly spaced, "nice" tick values across the domain.
scales.typeSignature: function getLinearTicks(domain: number[], count: number = 10)

scales.name: ScaleBindingOptions
scales.description: Low-level options for constructing a scale, providing conversion, inversion, inclusion, and tick generation callbacks.

scales.name: LinearScaleOptions
scales.description: Options shared by linear-based scales (continuous, logarithmic, power, etc.).

scales.name: scaleBand
scales.description: Creates a band scale that maps discrete domain values to evenly spaced bands within the range.

scales.name: BandScale
scales.description: A band scale that divides a continuous range into uniform bands for categorical data, exposing bandwidth and step.

scales.name: BandScaleOptions
scaleContinuous
scales.description: Creates a continuous linear scale that maps a numeric domain to a numeric range.

scales.name: scaleDiscrete
scales.description: Creates a discrete (ordinal) scale that maps domain values to corresponding range values by index.

scales.name: scaleDiverging
scales.description: Creates a diverging scale that maps values below and above a midpoint to separate sub-ranges.

scales.name: DivergingScaleOptions
scales.description: Options for a diverging scale, adding a midpoint to the base linear scale options.

scales.name: scaleLogarithmic
scales.description: Creates a logarithmic scale that maps a numeric domain to a range using a log transformation.

scales.name: scaleLog
LogarithmicScaleOptions
scales.description: Options for a logarithmic scale, adding a configurable base to the base linear scale options.

scales.name: scalePower
scales.description: Creates a power scale that maps a numeric domain to a range using an exponential transformation.

scales.name: PowerScaleOptions
scales.description: Options for a power scale, adding a configurable exponent to the base linear scale options.

scales.name: scaleSqrt
scales.description: Shortcut for a power scale with exponent 0.5 (square root).
scales.typeSignature: const scaleSqrt: (domain: number[], range: number[], options?: LinearScaleOptions) => Scale<number>

scales.name: scaleQuantile
scales.description: Creates a quantile scale that divides a sorted numeric domain into quantiles mapped to discrete range values.

scales.name: scaleQuantize
scales.description: Creates a quantize scale that divides a continuous numeric domain into uniform segments mapped to discrete range values.

scales.name: scaleThreshold
scales.description: Creates a threshold scale that maps numeric values to range values based on a set of threshold breakpoints.

scales.name: scaleTime
scales.description: Creates a time scale that maps a `Date` domain to a numeric range using linear interpolation of timestamps.

scales.name: ScaleMethod
scales.description: A function that maps a value from one space to another.
scales.typeSignature: type ScaleMethod<TInput = number, TOutput = number> = (value: TInput) => TOutput;

scales.name: Scale
scales.description: A callable scale with domain, range, inverse mapping, tick generation, and inclusion testing.
